lines.raw: Lesson 125 | 第125课
Tea for two | 两人喝茶

lines.english: Listen to the tape then answer this question.
lines.raw: Listen to the tape then answer this question. | 听录音，然后回答问题。

lines.english: Does Susan have tea by herself?
lines.raw: Does Susan have tea by herself? | 苏珊独自一人喝茶吗？

lines.english: Can't you come in and have tea now, Peter?
lines.chinese: 彼得，你现在不能进来喝茶吗？
lines.raw: Can't you come in and have tea now, Peter? | 彼得，你现在不能进来喝茶吗？
Not yet. | 还不能。

lines.english: I must water the garden first.
lines.raw: I must water the garden first. | 我必须先浇花园。

lines.english: Do you have to water it now?
lines.raw: Do you have to water it now? | 你现在非浇不可吗？
I'm afraid I must. | 恐怕非浇不可。
Look at it! | 你瞧！
It's terribly dry. | 干得厉害。
What a nuisance! | 真讨厌！

lines.english: Last summer it was very dry, too.
lines.raw: Last summer it was very dry, too. | 去年夏天也是很干。
Don't you remember? | 你不记得了吗？

lines.english: I had to water it every day.
lines.raw: I had to water it every day. | 我不得不每天浇水。

lines.english: Well, I'll have tea by myself.
lines.raw: Well, I'll have tea by myself. | 哎，我一个人喝茶了。
That was quick! | 真快！
Have you finished already? | 你已经浇完了吗？
Yes. | 是的。
Look out of the window. | 你往窗外看。
It's raining! | 下雨了！

lines.english: That means you don't need to water the garden.
lines.chinese: 这意味着你不用浇花园了。
lines.raw: That means you don't need to water the garden. | 这意味着你不用浇花园了。
That was a pleasant surprise. | 这真是个意外的惊喜。

lines.english: It means I can have tea, instead.
lines.chinese: 这意味着我可以去喝茶了。
lines.raw: It means I can have tea, instead. | 这意味着我可以去喝茶了。
